headline: 22 contract addresses discovered across Ethereum, BSC, Bitlayer, Base, Hemi, Core, and Goat chains; primary token contracts (bfBTC, bfUSD) and staking vaults documented
short_headline: 22 contracts across 7 EVM chains and Bitcoin network

findings.text: Block-explorer searches not performed — no explicit search queries against etherscan, bscscan, etc. name-tag feature executed.

Official deployments doc located at https://docs.bitfi.one/developer/bfbtc-overview/deployed-contracts and https://docs.bitfi.one/developer/bfusd-overview/deployed-contracts; lists mainnet and testnet contract addresses with roles.
Audit reports referenced: SlowMist audits for bfBTC and bfUSD linked on audit page; PDF content not fetched due to tool exhaustion.
GitHub repo at https://github.com/bitfi-labs contains 3 public repos (brand-assets, BitFi-gitbook, docs-vitepress) — no smart contract source code repository found.
Multi-chain enumeration: bfBTC deployed across 8 networks (Ethereum, BSC, Bitlayer, Hemi, Base, Core, Goat, TAC); bfUSD on Ethereum mainnet and Base Sepolia testnet.
No factory contracts identified in deployed contracts documentation; D6 cap-at-50 rule not applicable.
Role taxonomy applied: bfBTC and bfUSD classified as 'token'; hbfUSD and pbfUSD as 'vault'; minters and redeemers as 'other'.
All 22 addresses trace to fetched deployment documentation URLs.

rationale.verdict: Discovery catalogued 22 contract addresses across 7 mainnet EVM chains plus testnet deployments from official documentation. No factory-enumeration or GitHub commit-pinned deployment artifacts executed; audit PDF scope tables not retrieved due to tool constraints. Address book now populated for downstream slice reads.

evidence.shows: BitFi protocol homepage confirming protocol name, product names (bfBTC, bfUSD), and links to documentation.
BitFi documentation site with Developer section listing deployed contracts and audit reports.

bfBTC mainnet deployed contract addresses on 8 networks: BSC (0x623F2774d9f27B59bc6b954544487532CE79d9DF), Bitlayer (0xcdfb58c8c859cb3f62ebe9cf2767f9e036c7fb15), Hemi (0x623F2774d9f27B59bc6b954544487532CE79d9DF), Ethereum (0xCdFb58c8C859Cb3F62ebe9Cf2767F9e036C7fb15), Base (0x623F2774d9f27B59bc6b954544487532CE79d9DF), Core (0xCdFb58c8C859Cb3F62ebe9Cf2767F9e036C7fb15), Goat (0x623F2774d9f27B59bc6b954544487532CE79d9DF), TAC (0x623F2774d9f27B59bc6b954544487532CE79d9DF); plus testnet addresses on Ethereum Sepolia, Bitlayer Testnet, Base Sepolia, Core Testnet2, Goat Testnet.

bfUSD mainnet deployed on Ethereum: token at 0xa3eB7A9e57FCa4e40b79E394eD5eB37fEd205A24; Horizon vault (hbfUSD) 0xde5D4ab42251Ba9AF6f247CF07c9a4793fA6eD88; Pulsar vault (pbfUSD) 0x4f85cBfDefBdb3fA96fCf1E38b0ee68dB9Ae438b; Minters (USDT: 0xa3dEaa934a4CC648a45d8269a012894315e53518, USDC: 0x0497DD14524dD59Bcd76da7DC977A0f6120b1AcA); Redeemers (Instant: 0x0971cB672b4eF3E19284Aa64717aFb154A6fbeDF, Standard: 0x1898f16583152a6af129e3df14f0C955ca6201f7); Zap: 0x5506570fD527c06902b51e33535943C9DC941BbE. Testnet on Base Sepolia also listed.
SlowMist audit reports referenced for both bfBTC and bfUSD with links to PDF downloads and SlowMist GitHub repo.
BitFi Labs GitHub organization with 3 public repositories: brand-assets, BitFi-gitbook, docs-vitepress. No smart contract source code repository found.

unknowns: D1: block-explorer name-tag searches not performed due to tool limitations; address discovery relied solely on official documentation.
D3: audit PDF scope tables not fetched — SlowMist reports exist but content (contract lists, addresses in scope, audit date) not retrieved.
D4: GitHub smart contract repository not located; only docs/branding repos present in BitFi-Labs org.
D6-offchain: no factory contract discovery attempted; factorychild enumeration N/A based on current documentation.

protocol_metadata.about: BitFi is an on-chain asset management platform providing CeDeFi yields through two primary products: bfBTC, a yield-bearing liquid staking token for Bitcoin enabling users to earn returns from mirror trading and DeFi strategies while maintaining redeemability for native BTC across multiple chains; and bfUSD, a stablecoin backed by USDC/USDT with two staking pools (Horizon for low-risk 9% APY and Pulsar for higher-yield 17% APY). The protocol combines institutional-grade CeFi infrastructure through Ceffu custody with DeFi composability, deploying assets into delta-neutral quantitative strategies coordinated via Binance's MirrorX engine.
